skill.name: setup
skill.description: Project setup guidance for adding shadcn UI components.
skill.summary: Quick reference for adding shadcn UI components to the project.
skill.author: Emz1998

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged a weak cryptographic algorithm at SKILL.md:3, but that line is only the frontmatter description for setup documentation. I found no cryptographic code, prompt injection text, network access, environment access, or executable script behavior in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The static finding reported a weak cryptographic algorithm at SKILL.md:3. That line contains only the description metadata for setup instructions, so there is no cryptographic implementation or unsafe algorithm to confirm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is plain YAML frontmatter text. No crypto API, hash algorithm, cipher, or security-sensitive code appears in the reviewed skill files.

content.value_statement: Teams need a clear reference for adding shared UI components without searching through project notes. This skill provides concise setup guidance for using the shadcn component command in the project.
actual_capabilities: Explains that the skill is for project setup guidance.
Points users to the shadcn UI component workflow.
Provides the documented command format for adding a component.
References the included shadcn components note.
Helps contributors ask focused setup questions.
limitations: Does not install dependencies or run commands for the user.
Does not list completed shadcn components because the component note is empty.
Does not provide full project setup beyond the documented shadcn guidance.
Does not verify whether a target project already has shadcn configured.

use_cases.title: New Contributor Setup
use_cases.description: A new contributor uses the skill to understand the documented way to add UI components.

use_cases.title: Component Addition Reference
use_cases.description: A developer checks the expected command format before adding a shadcn component.

use_cases.title: Setup Documentation Review
use_cases.description: A maintainer reviews the current setup notes and identifies where more detail is needed.

prompt_templates.title: Ask Setup Purpose
prompt_templates.prompt: What setup guidance does this skill provide?
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner user wants a quick summary of the skill.

prompt_templates.title: Add One Component
prompt_templates.prompt: How should I add a shadcn UI component to this project?
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer needs the documented component workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Check Component Notes
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the shadcn component notes and tell me what information is currently documented.
prompt_templates.scenario: Contributor wants to inspect the available component reference.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Setup Docs
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the existing setup notes to suggest missing documentation that would help new frontend contributors.
prompt_templates.scenario: Maintainer wants to expand sparse setup guidance.

output_examples.input: What does this setup skill cover?
output_examples.output: It covers basic project setup guidance for adding shadcn UI components. The current documentation is short and focuses on the component add workflow.

output_examples.input: What should I know before adding a component?
output: Confirm the project already uses shadcn UI.
Choose the component name you need.
Run the documented shadcn add command in your project terminal.
Review the generated files before committing changes.

best_practices: Confirm the target component name before using the add command.
Review generated component files and dependencies before committing.
Keep the component notes updated when project conventions change.
anti_patterns: Assuming this skill contains full application setup instructions.
Adding components without checking project conventions first.
Treating the empty component note as a complete component inventory.

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It provides concise setup guidance for adding shadcn UI components to the project.

faq.question: Does this skill run installation commands?
faq.answer: No. It documents the workflow, but users must run any commands themselves.

faq.question: Does it include a complete component list?
faq.answer: No. The included component note is currently empty and does not provide a complete inventory.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, or Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Is this a general shadcn UI guide?
faq.answer: No. It is a short project-specific setup note, not full shadcn documentation.

faq.question: What should maintainers add next?
faq.answer: Maintainers should add project prerequisites, accepted components, and any local styling conventions.
